caveats: a: BLS evidence is broader than six-digit 337920.
a: Already-automated cutting and online configuration are excluded from the remaining opportunity.
rho: AI-tool use is not implemented substitution.
rho: Visualization and content tools may increase conversion rather than remove labor.
e: The provided count uses a machinery EBITDA margin that may not match window-covering economics.
e: Vertically integrated dealer-installers can be difficult to classify between manufacturing and services.
s5: No six-digit qualifying-transfer denominator was found.
s5: Succession resilience is not a transaction probability.
q: CPSC requirements create compliance obligations but do not directly measure pricing power.
q: Capture should be higher in custom motorized or specified systems and lower in commodity stock blinds.
d5: The market figures are North American, nominal, and commercially produced.
d5: Safety rules may shift product mix and price rather than total quantity.
d5: New construction and renovation channels can diverge.
o: An accountable operator may be offshore even when the physical product remains necessary.
o: Smart glazing could remove some future coverings but is unlikely to replace the current basket broadly within five years.

lens.customer_and_revenue_model: Made-to-measure dealer and designer orders, builder and commercial projects, private-label or retail programs, e-commerce direct sales, and replacement purchases, generally priced per opening, unit, room package, or project.
lens.excluded_activities: Captive internal shops, shells, curtains and draperies, shutters classified elsewhere, installation-only dealers, imported-product resellers without manufacturing, and window-film contractors.
lens.included_activities: US lower-middle-market manufacturers repeatedly supplying stock or custom venetian and other blinds, roller, cellular, Roman and other shades, curtain and drapery rods and poles, fixtures, and motorized window coverings to external dealers, designers, retailers, builders, commercial customers, and direct consumers.

a.bridge: BLS reports occupations for broader furniture manufacturing, and NIST identifies manufacturing AI uses in inspection, maintenance, forecasting, and process decisions. I map high exposure to measurement intake, configuration, quoting, order entry, scheduling, purchasing, marketing, documentation, and customer service; partial exposure to design, pattern nesting, quality, and supervision; and low exposure to fabric or slat cutting, sewing, rail assembly, motor integration, finishing, packing, and maintenance execution. Broad furniture data include woodworking and upholstery and may misstate this product mix.
caveats: BLS evidence is broader than six-digit 337920.
Already-automated cutting and online configuration are excluded from the remaining opportunity.
a.change_evidence: A wage-weighted task inventory across 10-12 independent blind and shade plants, including time studies for measurement validation, configuration, quoting, cutting, sewing, assembly, motorization, inspection, and service, would materially change the range.
a.rationale: Made-to-measure configuration and order checking create digital opportunity, but production and motor or hardware assembly remain physical and many configurators are already partly automated.

d5.bridge: A commercial market source values North American window coverings at $5.65 billion in 2025 and $5.92 billion in 2026, citing smart-home adoption, while CPSC requirements push stock products toward cordless, short, inaccessible, or motorized controls. I use market growth and product-standard change as proxies, then temper them to constant-price five-year US demand for the current blind, shade, rod, and fixture basket. Market value can rise through premium motorization without equal unit growth, and a global market publisher may overstate domestic demand.
caveats: The market figures are North American, nominal, and commercially produced.
Safety rules may shift product mix and price rather than total quantity.
New construction and renovation channels can diverge.
d5.change_evidence: Inflation-adjusted US manufacturer unit shipments by product and channel, residential renovation and housing completion forecasts, commercial window area, motorization penetration, and replacement-age surveys would materially change the range.
d5.rationale: Renovation, aging building stock, privacy, glare and thermal control, safety replacement, and smart or motorized products support demand. Housing cycles, office weakness, imports, do-it-yourself installation, durable products, and alternative curtains or films create downside.

caveats: The provided count uses a machinery EBITDA margin that may not match window-covering economics.
Vertically integrated dealer-installers can be difficult to classify between manufacturing and services.
e.change_evidence: Resolve all 61 estimated in-band firms for actual US manufacturing, ultimate ownership, imported content, external repeat revenue, dealer and customer concentration, normalized EBITDA, safety compliance, and owner dependence.
e.rationale: Many in-band operators repeatedly manufacture custom or stock products for external channels and have transferable equipment, patterns, brands, dealer relationships, configurators, and motorization expertise. Import-only resellers, installation-heavy dealers, captive units, owner-dependent custom shops, distress, concentration, and errors from the machinery-margin bridge reduce eligibility.

l.detail: QCEW 2024 private total annual wages at 337920 ($526,899,402) x benefits multiplier 1.4294 [ECEC private-industry total comp $46.60/hr / wages $32.60/hr = 1.4294 (March 2026 reference period)] / SUSB 2022 receipts at 337920 ($2,476,776,000). Vintage mismatch: 2024 wages over 2022 receipts, stated. Level: 6-digit. Harmonized to IPS basis ÷1.3407 (median of 65 dual-method codes).

n.detail: margin 0.1962 (margins.json[33]: Damodaran Jan 2026: Machinery EBITDA/Sales 19.62%); revenue/employee $214,310 (SUSB 2022 receipts/employment at 337920); class <5: 145 firms, avg revenue/firm $410,959 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $80,630 -> out of $1-10M band; class 5-9: 47 firms, avg revenue/firm $1,923,106 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $377,313 -> out of $1-10M band; class 10-19: 39 firms, avg revenue/firm $2,274,615 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $446,280 -> out of $1-10M band; class 20-99: 51 firms, avg revenue/firm $7,733,706 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $1,517,353 -> IN $1-10M band; class 100-499: 10 firms, avg revenue/firm $33,866,100 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $6,644,529 -> IN $1-10M band; class 500+: 10 firms, avg revenue/firm $150,501,100 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $29,528,316 -> out of $1-10M band; n_band = 61

caveats: An accountable operator may be offshore even when the physical product remains necessary.
Smart glazing could remove some future coverings but is unlikely to replace the current basket broadly within five years.
o.change_evidence: Architect, dealer, builder, and consumer interviews plus smart-glass, window-film, import, do-it-yourself, and replacement-cycle data would quantify elimination and self-service.
o.rationale: Customers still require accountable manufacturers to configure, cut, sew or form, assemble, integrate controls, safety-test, package, and warrant physical blinds and shades. Software and smart controls enhance rather than replace the covering; limited loss can come from window films, electrochromic glass, curtains, imported finished products, customer self-assembly, and longer product life.

caveats: CPSC requirements create compliance obligations but do not directly measure pricing power.
Capture should be higher in custom motorized or specified systems and lower in commodity stock blinds.
q.change_evidence: Dealer, builder, e-commerce, and commercial contracts plus five-year order-level histories for list-to-net price, commissions, rebates, remakes, warranty, freight, installation credits, and gross margin would reveal pass-through.
q.rationale: Made-to-measure fit, design, fabrics, motorization integration, dealer service, short lead time, remake support, brand, and safety compliance can preserve savings. Online comparison, big-box retail, dealer commissions, designer discounts, builder bids, imports, and promotional pricing share benefits over five years.

rho.bridge: NIST reports 46% manufacturing AI-tool use and more than 80% expecting increased use within two years, while listing data quality, cost, skills, cybersecurity, and legacy integration barriers. I translate broad adoption into five-year implementation of exposed tasks. General manufacturing may overstate small fabricators' data maturity, while e-commerce, image, configuration, document, forecasting, and service tools are readily accessible.
caveats: AI-tool use is not implemented substitution.
Visualization and content tools may increase conversion rather than remove labor.
rho.change_evidence: Comparable-company deployment records showing baseline hours, remake rates, measurement errors, configurator integration, false rejects, customer adoption, and sustained labor savings would materially move the range.
rho.rationale: Order validation, measurement anomaly checks, visualization, schedules, service triage, and inspection can deploy, but custom openings, field-measurement errors, varied textiles and hardware, safety requirements, dealer-system integration, and limited IT capacity constrain implementation.

s5.bridge: Deloitte surveyed 500 US family-enterprise members and found only 24% of current-generation respondents strongly agreed their succession plan could withstand departure of an important family employee. I use this as broad succession-pressure evidence, then reduce potential exits for internal transfers, non-control investments, closures, and failed sales. The survey is not window-covering-specific or a timing measure, while channel consolidation can increase strategic acquisitions.
caveats: No six-digit qualifying-transfer denominator was found.
Succession resilience is not a transaction probability.
s5.change_evidence: Owner-age and intended-exit outreach to resolved manufacturers plus five years of closed control deals from PitchBook, GF Data, Axial, WCMA members, and home-products advisers would narrow the range.
s5.rationale: Regional fabricators, brands, and dealer networks are plausible succession or add-on targets, but owner relationships, measurement and installation dependence, imports, and concentration can impair transferability.

principal_driver: Custom configuration plus smart, safe, energy-managing products supports recurring physical demand.
principal_weakness: Transparent retail channels and imports limit value capture despite customization.

sources.supports: Defines venetian and other blinds and shades, curtain and drapery rods and poles, and related fixtures.
sources.title: Census NAICS definition: 337920 Blind and Shade Manufacturing

sources.supports: Provides occupation-level employment and wages for broader furniture industries, including sewing, upholstery, finishing, machine operation, and other production work.
sources.title: BLS May 2023 occupation estimates for Furniture and Related Product Manufacturing

sources.supports: Reports 46% manufacturing AI-tool use and more than 80% expecting increased use in two years, with operational use cases and adoption barriers.
sources.title: The Rise of Artificial Intelligence in U.S. Manufacturing

sources.supports: States that noncompliant window coverings are a substantial product hazard and requires stock operating cords to be cordless, short, or inaccessible, with inner-cord requirements for stock and custom products.
sources.title: CPSC Window Coverings 15(j) Rule Business Guidance

sources.supports: Reports North American market value of $5.65 billion in 2025 and projected $5.92 billion in 2026, citing smart-home and smart-covering adoption; used only as a commercial demand proxy.
sources.title: Window Covering Market overview

sources.supports: Describes a January 2024 survey of 500 US family-enterprise respondents and reports that 24% of current-generation respondents strongly agreed their succession plan would withstand departure of an important family employee.
sources.title: Deloitte Private survey: generational disparities in family-business succession planning

title: Blind and Shade Manufacturing
